goal: Build a 2-robot relay-stacker: Robot A and Robot B share one conveyor and one pallet. A picks Cube_1 from the conveyor and places it at z=0.825 on the pallet (bottom layer). B picks Cube_2 and places it at z=0.875 ON TOP of A's cube. Combines CP-02's multi-robot scoping (per-robot _curobo_pp_sub_<TAG>) with CP-13's column-stack drop_targets pattern.
thoughts:
Six 2-robot-relay-stacker specifics for CP-14 (extends CP-02 + CP-13):
1) 2 Frankas at x=±0.6, y=0 (closer than CP-02's ±1.0 because we share one pallet behind them at y=-0.4). Each robot oriented [0.7071, 0, 0, 0.7071] (facing +Y).
2) Each robot picks ONE cube — A picks Cube_1, B picks Cube_2 — by scoping setup_pick_place_controller's source_paths. Controller's _next_cube only searches its own source_paths.
3) Pallet at [0, -0.4, 0.775] (centered behind both robots, bbox xy [-0.075, -0.475] → [0.075, -0.325]). Reachable by both robots: dist 0.6+0.4 = 0.72m horizontal — within 0.85m reach.
4) drop_targets per controller: A's drop_targets={'/World/Cube_1': [0,-0.4,0.825]}, B's drop_targets={'/World/Cube_2': [0,-0.4,0.875]}. Same xy, different z.
5) Per-robot subscription: cuRobo handler scopes _SUB_ATTR by robot path. A → _curobo_pp_sub_World_FrankaA, B → _curobo_pp_sub_World_FrankaB. Coexist without interference (CP-02 pattern verified).
6) Sensor placement: separate sensors per robot at each robot's natural pick zone. A's sensor at (-0.2, 0.4, 0.835), B's at (0.2, 0.4, 0.835). Belt motion carries cubes left-to-right; A picks first, then B picks next.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + parent Xform
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Wider table for 2 robots
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Robot A — left side
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/FrankaA",
    position=[-0.6, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)
# Robot B — right side
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/FrankaB",
    position=[ 0.6, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Single conveyor in front of both robots (y=0.4)
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 2 cubes — Cube_1 will be picked by Robot A (left), Cube_2 by Robot B (right).
# Cube_1 starts further left so belt carries it past A first.
for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -0.8]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(2)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(2)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Shared pallet — between the two robots, in their reach
create_prim(prim_path="/World/PalletBase", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, -0.4, 0.775], scale=[0.075, 0.075, 0.025])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/PalletBase", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Per-robot pick sensors
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/SensorA", position=[-0.2, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/SensorB", position=[0.2, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Robot A controller — picks Cube_1, places at base layer (z=0.825)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaA",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/SensorA",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_1"],
    destination_path="/World/PalletBase",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_1": [0.0, -0.4, 0.825],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/PalletBase"],
)

# Robot B controller — picks Cube_2, places at top layer (z=0.875)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/FrankaB",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/SensorB",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_2"],
    destination_path="/World/PalletBase",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_2": [0.0, -0.4, 0.875],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/PalletBase"],
)
extension_notes: CP-14 combines CP-02's multi-robot architecture with CP-13's drop_targets stack pattern. Each robot has independent source_paths + drop_targets. Two _curobo_pp_sub_<TAG> subscriptions coexist. Future variants: 3+ robots cooperative stacking; alternating-color pallet (robot A places red, robot B places blue); mirror-pose paired-pick.
failure_modes: Both robots try to pick same cube — source_paths scoping prevents this. Robot A only sees Cube_1, Robot B only sees Cube_2.

Robot B picks before Robot A finishes — they're independent. Robot B may pick Cube_2 at the same time A is transit. Could cause collision over the shared PalletBase. CP-02's solution: planning_obstacles include each others' bodies, but cuRobo doesn't dynamically include robot links. Acceptable for v1 — robots are 1.2m apart, transit doesn't cross.
Both controllers race for same _curobo_pp_sub_ name — fixed in CP-02 era: per-robot scoping by robot path tag.
Pallet too small — 15x15cm gives ~5cm margin around 5cm cubes. Robot drop precision typically 5cm; cubes may drop just outside. Future: enlarge to 20-30cm.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (multi-cube cube_paths + Mode A 3-strike fix)
